place: Edmeston CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Edmeston takes its name from brothers Robert and William Edmeston, early landowners granted 10,000 acres after the 1768 Treaty of Fort Stanwix.
The Unadilla River marks Edmeston’s western edge, while elevations climb from about 1,100 to over 1,900 feet across its rolling hills.
New York State Route 80 cuts through Edmeston’s southeastern corner, a historic east‑west corridor that still serves regional traffic.
The Edmeston branch of the New York, Ontario & Western Railroad operated from 1889 until 1960, linking the town to broader rail networks before its abandonment.
Kraft opened a cheese plant in South Edmeston in 1920, which later became the first Chobani Greek‑yogurt facility in 2007.
Since the nineteenth century, Edmeston has been a center of dairy farming and cheese production, a tradition that shaped its rural character.
